famous quote:
"The more we let God take us over, the more truly ourselves we become - because He made us. He invented us. He invented all the different people that you and I were intended to be. . .It is when I turn to Christ, when I give up myself to His personality, that I first begin to have a real personality of my own."
-- C.S. Lewis
Interview Text:
Bayo:

(part 3)

I ask him, "Can you tell me about faith in America verses Africa?"
In Africa it is much different. I Raised a boy from the dead and when I tell people back in New York they are skeptical and ask me questions like "Are you sure he wasn't just epileptic, or that he just fainted?" It is because in Africa they must believe in God. They don't have the government that will pay them welfare if they can't support themselves. Much of the time they can only depend on God. In America you don't really starve.
Music Video Instructions: Does this describe our constant pursuit of God?
